comment: Build-correctness and rendered-workflow gate: zero-dependency unit tests over the DOM-free episode/preset model, a preview-build smoke check, a headless Chrome rendered workflow that uploads three generated local WebM files through the shipped Host/Guest controls and decodes blob-backed pixels in the composed preview across Split/Stack/Spotlight — including per-region Stack row pixels and Spotlight host-dominant/PiP geometry — surviving preset switches with export still enabled, a headless social-context workflow that enters distinct per-speaker social links through the real inputs and confirms they store per speaker, surface as derived names in the preview, and survive a preset switch with the uploads intact, and a headless export workflow that uploads two generated videos, enters social links, chooses a preset, clicks the real Export action, and confirms the produced file is a genuinely playable video (loads back into a <video> with real dimensions and non-trivial bytes) reflecting the selected preset and carrying audible mixed speaker audio, then re-exports a second time in the same session across a preset switch (no reload or re-upload) and confirms that second file is also playable and still carries non-silent audio, and a headless custom-layout workflow that opens the layout editor from Stack and Spotlight, cancels after unsaved Host edits while confirming the chosen preset, live preview, uploads, and export readiness are preserved, then resizes and drags the Host frame with real mouse events, confirms the live preview renders the moved Host video, saves the arrangement as a named reusable template, confirms the applied template renders the saved positions and survives a preset round-trip, and exports a genuinely playable video while the custom template is selected, a headless visual-moments workflow that uploads two generated speaker videos through the normal Host/Guest controls, enters social links, chooses Split, adds a timed title moment (0:00-0:03) and callout moment (0:04-0:07) through the real moments UI, verifies by sampling canvas pixels during playback and while scrubbing that each moment renders only inside its scheduled range (and neither in the gap), confirms the same moments stay attached and render over Stack and Spotlight, then clicks the real Export action, loads the produced file back into a video element, and seeks to 1.5s/3.5s/5.5s to confirm the title and callout overlays are burned into nonblank exported frames only at their scheduled times, and a headless template-persistence workflow that uploads three generated speaker videos, customizes a layout by moving/resizing the Host and Guest 1 frames through the real editor, saves it as a named reusable show template, then performs a genuine full-page reload (same browser profile, fresh JS realm) and confirms the reloaded episode starts with no leftover media while the saved template survived, uploads a brand-new set of speaker videos, selects the persisted template, confirms the preview renders the new videos at the saved frame arrangement with export enabled, confirms the template stays selected and correctly positioned across a preset round-trip, and exports a genuinely playable video while the persisted template is active, and a headless template-apply workflow that uploads two generated speaker videos, customizes and saves a template with a visibly-distinct Host position, clicks the product's own "Start new episode" control (no page reload) and confirms the fresh episode has no leftover media/social links while the saved template survived unselected, uploads a brand-new pair of speaker videos, confirms the preview does NOT already show the old custom geometry before a template is chosen, selects the saved template from the normal setup/preset controls without reopening the canvas editor, confirms the new videos render at the saved Host position, and exports a genuinely playable video with that geometry burned in. No seeded product media, committed fixture output, or verifier-only product paths are used. Runs in the maintainer's browser-capable sandbox image (Node 22 + Chromium); each browser check resolves CHROME_BIN from the image's playwright install when not already set.

checks.name: rendered-preview
checks.run: CHROME_BIN="${CHROME_BIN:-$(find /ms-playwright -maxdepth 3 -type f -name chrome -path '*chrome-linux*' 2>/dev/null | head -n1)}" node scripts/verify-rendered-preview.mjs

checks.name: rendered-social
checks.run: CHROME_BIN="${CHROME_BIN:-$(find /ms-playwright -maxdepth 3 -type f -name chrome -path '*chrome-linux*' 2>/dev/null | head -n1)}" node scripts/verify-social-context.mjs

checks.name: rendered-export
checks.run: CHROME_BIN="${CHROME_BIN:-$(find /ms-playwright -maxdepth 3 -type f -name chrome -path '*chrome-linux*' 2>/dev/null | head -n1)}" node scripts/verify-export.mjs

checks.name: rendered-custom-layout
checks.run: CHROME_BIN="${CHROME_BIN:-$(find /ms-playwright -maxdepth 3 -type f -name chrome -path '*chrome-linux*' 2>/dev/null | head -n1)}" node scripts/verify-custom-layout.mjs

checks.name: visual-moments
checks.run: CHROME_BIN="${CHROME_BIN:-$(find /ms-playwright -maxdepth 3 -type f -name chrome -path '*chrome-linux*' 2>/dev/null | head -n1)}" node scripts/verify-visual-moments.mjs

checks.name: template-persistence
checks.run: CHROME_BIN="${CHROME_BIN:-$(find /ms-playwright -maxdepth 3 -type f -name chrome -path '*chrome-linux*' 2>/dev/null | head -n1)}" node scripts/verify-template-persistence.mjs

checks.name: template-apply
checks.run: CHROME_BIN="${CHROME_BIN:-$(find /ms-playwright -maxdepth 3 -type f -name chrome -path '*chrome-linux*' 2>/dev/null | head -n1)}" node scripts/verify-template-apply.mjs
